phase: Phase P2 — OS Keychain & Secure Provider Vault

quality_gates.name: OS Keychain Storage
quality_gates.description: All provider keys stored exclusively in OS keychain. Zero file-based, env-based, or browser-storage fallbacks. ICredentialProvider interface implemented with BLOCK fallback policy.

quality_gates.name: Ephemeral Injection
quality_gates.description: Keys injected into runtime via stdin or isolated env. Desktop memory cleared within 5 seconds. forceClearInjection for immediate revocation.

quality_gates.name: Provider Settings UI
quality_gates.description: Full provider management UI: masked key input, edit/save/rotate/delete actions, connection test indicator.

quality_gates.name: IPC + Gateway Integration
quality_gates.description: 3 new IPC commands (register_provider, test_provider_connection, get_gateway_routing_status) with type-safe stubs. Gateway failover contract (429/5xx triggers failover_ready).

quality_gates.name: Security Tests (15+ assertions)
quality_gates.description: 5 test suites with 15+ assertions: keychain-only storage, ephemeral clear ≤5s, no log leaks, rotation/revocation, gateway failover contract.

quality_gates.name: Leak-Free & Zero-Persistence
quality_gates.description: No apiKey in console.log, telemetry payloads, or network headers confirmed by contract tests. No .env, localStorage, or file-system I/O in credential provider.

quality_gates.name: Zero Core Mutations
quality_gates.description: All P2 changes isolated to emo-desktop/product-layer. Zero modifications to core/runtime/*, core/orchestration/*, core/memory/*.

summary: All 7 quality gates PASSED. Phase P2 delivers OS-native credential management, ephemeral injection with ≤5s auto-clear, provider management UI, gateway routing/failover IPC, and 15+ security contract tests — all with zero core mutations.
